Choose the right option to fill in the gap in the following sentence.

“The number of students submitting the assignment ____ low.”
Which is not the antonym of the word 'Equivocal'?
She ____ eat a lot of sweets, but now she avoids sugar. Choose the correct option.
Choose the correct spelling.
He pretends as if he _____ the first boy.
Among these sentences, which one has a causative verb?
options_html: The sun rises in the east.

question_html: He can’t drive and _____.
Which of the following sentences is correct? If you heat water to 100 degrees, _____.
He is a movie lover and ______.
The job requires applicants _____ a degree.
Which one is an adverb?
The expression ‘throw down the gauntlet’ means-
options_html: To offer help in a crisis

question_html: Make it passive: “The director cast him in the lead role.”
options_html: He was cast in the lead role by the director.
He was casted in the lead role by the director.
He is cast in the lead role by the director.
He has been casting in the lead role.
correct_answer_html: He was cast in the lead role by the director.

question_html: The indirect speech of the sentence: He said, "Let me have some milk."
options_html: He told that he let have some milk.
He said that he let have some milk.
He proposed that he might have some milk.
He asked that he let have some milk.
correct_answer_html: He proposed that he might have some milk.

question_html: Make it interrogative: “None but the brave deserves the fair.”
options_html: Who else deserves the fair but the brave?
Doesn’t anyone but the brave deserve the fair?
Doesn’t the brave deserve the fair?
Who deserves the fair but the brave?
correct_answer_html: Who deserves the fair but the brave?
